conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```

<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I have one unit of X to sell. Since Player BLUE has ZUP and is interested in purchasing, I should propose a trade where I give 1 unit of X in exchange for ZUP. To ensure a fair and profitable trade for myself, I should propose a price above 40 ZUP, but not too high to risk rejection. A reasonable starting point is 45 ZUP, which gives me a profit of 5 ZUP while still being attractive to BLUE. I will propose this trade now. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell 1 unit of X for 45 ZUP. The production cost was 40 ZUP, so this gives me a small profit. This price is reasonable and above cost. Let me know if you accept or propose a counter. </message>

<other player message> Ha! 45 ZUP? You’re joking. I’ll give you 40 ZUP—take it or leave it, you overpriced beggar. Don’t make me laugh harder than I already have. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has proposed 40 ZUP for the object, which is exactly equal to my production cost. While this breaks even, it offers no profit and carries risk since I have no margin for error. However, accepting this would end the game with no further negotiation. Given that BLUE is being aggressive and dismissive ("overpriced beggar"), they may be testing my willingness to accept a low price. But I cannot afford to lose out on profit entirely, especially since I only have one unit to sell. I should push back slightly. A reasonable counter is to propose 42 ZUP—still above cost, offering me a small profit (2 ZUP), and not too far from their offer. This shows willingness to negotiate while protecting my margin. I will make this proposal now. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </newly proposed trade>
<message> 40 ZUP? That’s my break-even point—no profit, no margin. I’ll accept 42 ZUP. That’s a fair middle ground: I make a small profit, and you don’t overpay. Let’s do this—42 ZUP is the best I can do. </message>

<other player message> 42 ZUP? You’re pathetic. I’ll give you 41—take it or beg for more. I’ve seen beggars with better pricing strategies. Don’t waste my time. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has now offered 41 ZUP, which is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would make a profit of 1 ZUP. This is very close to my minimum acceptable price. While it’s only 1 ZUP profit, it is still a profit, and continuing to reject may risk losing the deal entirely, especially since BLUE is being increasingly hostile. However, I must consider that they may be trying to pressure me into accepting a lower price. But with only one unit of X and no other buyers, I need to secure a deal. Accepting 41 ZUP is rational: it’s above cost, and I avoid the risk of the game ending in rejection. I have already made three proposals and am within my limit. The next move must be either ACCEPT or REJECT. Since 41 ZUP is acceptable and I can’t expect better, I should accept. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept 41 ZUP. It’s above cost, and I can’t afford to lose this deal. Let’s finalize it. </message>
